title: The Pillar Of Fame.
content:
Fame's pillar here, at last, we set,
    Outduring    marble,    brass,    or    jet.
    Charm'd     and     enchanted    so
    As    to     withstand    the    blow
         Of o v e r t h r o w;
         Nor shall    the    seas,
         Or    o u t r a g e s
         Of    storms    o'erbear
         What    we    uprear.
         Tho'    kingdoms    fall,
    This    pillar    never    shall
    Decline    or    waste    at    all;
    But    stand    for    ever    by    his    own
    Firm    and    well-fix'd    foundation.
meaning:
Exploring the themes of classic, Robert Herrick delivers a powerful performance in "The Pillar Of Fame."...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
